title: The Shattered Toast at Whitby Hall
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Whitmore, renowned art dealer, was found dead in the dining room, its polished oak table set with a glass, a bottle, a plate and a knife. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Loxley
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Dr. Felix Armitage helped me set the glass down on the side table. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Miranda Havers and Eleanor Whitmore were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Dr. Felix Armitage and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Miranda Havers
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the knife and did not go near the bottle.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Dr. Felix Armitage
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, I helped Gareth Loxley set the glass down on the side table. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Loxley and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: The Edge of Knife
clues.detail: A fresh line of blood runs along one edge of the knife, and its point has a new bend. Eleanor Whitmore has a narrow wound consistent with that edge.

clues.title: Ring beneath Glass
clues.detail: The fresh circular ring beneath the glass is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Loxley and Dr. Felix Armitage both state that they set the glass down on the side table at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Knife
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle of the knife. Miranda Havers's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Bottle
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged inside the handle joint of the bottle on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Miranda Havers has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Plate
clues.detail: A crack runs across the plate. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Miranda Havers killed Eleanor Whitmore because the imminent exposure of his embezzlement scheme.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitmore was fatally stabbed with the knife.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Miranda Havers claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Gareth Loxley and Dr. Felix Armitage left together at 8:45 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the knife and the black and gold fragment recovered from the bottle place Miranda Havers inside afterwards. Miranda Havers therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitmore and reach the knife during the murder window.
